knownFor: Kamarag, the bellicose Klingon ambassador who demands Kirk's extradition before the Federation Council.
reveal: The blustering Klingon diplomat is American actor John Schuck, familiar from McMillan & Wife and as Herman Munster on The Munsters Today.

knownFor: English actor (EastEnders, True Blood, Pacific Rim).
reveal: The towering, tusked orc Orgrim is Robert Kazinsky in ILM performance capture.

knownFor: American actor (Back to the Future's George McFly, Willard).
reveal: The pustuled, misshapen monster Grendel is Crispin Glover under Robert Zemeckis's performance capture.

knownFor: Oscar-winning English stage and screen actor (Bridge of Spies, Wolf Hall).
reveal: Spielberg's Big Friendly Giant is Mark Rylance, his face and performance captured and enlarged by Weta.

knownFor: Australian comedian, creator of Dame Edna Everage.
reveal: The grotesque, goitered Great Goblin king is Barry Humphries in motion capture and voice.

knownFor: English actor (Q in the James Bond films, A Very English Scandal).
reveal: The voice of the fully CG, marmalade-loving bear Paddington is Ben Whishaw.

knownFor: American actor (Colonel Quaritch in Avatar, the blind man in Don't Breathe).
reveal: In the sequel Quaritch returns as a nine-foot blue Na'vi recombinant — entirely Stephen Lang in mocap.

knownFor: Cherokee actor (The Last of the Mohicans, Dances with Wolves, Heat).
reveal: The Omaticaya clan leader Eytukan is Wes Studi, fully transformed into a Na'vi.

knownFor: English actress (In America, Minority Report, The Walking Dead).
reveal: The compassionate green Martian Sola is Samantha Morton under full performance capture.

knownFor: Northern Irish actor (Rome, Mance Rayder in Game of Thrones, Belfast).
reveal: The horned alien invader Steppenwolf is Ciarán Hinds in motion capture and voice.

knownFor: South African actor (Wikus in District 9, Elysium, The A-Team).
reveal: The childlike sentient police robot Chappie is Sharlto Copley in a mocap suit acting opposite the cast on set.

knownFor: The sky pirate Balthier in Final Fantasy XII and Fenris in Dragon Age II.
reveal: The self-appointed leading man of Final Fantasy XII is voiced by Gideon Emery. Away from Ivalice, the same actor gave Dragon Age II's Fenris his tightly controlled anger.

knownFor: Nero in Devil May Cry, plus Ichigo, Vash and the Adam Park Black/Green Power Ranger.
reveal: American actor and musician Johnny Yong Bosch.

knownFor: The Horseman War in Darksiders, plus Gollum in Shadow of Mordor and Illidan Stormrage in World of Warcraft.
reveal: American voice actor, writer and director Liam O'Brien, a Critical Role cast member.

knownFor: The most iconic figure in lucha libre history, a silver-masked hero who starred in 50-plus films and never showed his face in public for nearly half a century.
reveal: Rodolfo Guzmán Huerta (1917–1984), who kept his face hidden even from friends and unmasked on television only a week before he died.

knownFor: The 'Man of a Thousand Masks' who globalized lucha libre and entered the WWE Hall of Fame, never unmasked in the ring.
reveal: Aarón Rodríguez Arellano (b. 1942), who to this day conceals his true face.

knownFor: The silver-masked heir to El Santo's legend, carrying his father's mask across four decades of lucha libre.
reveal: Jorge Ernesto Guzmán Rodríguez (b. 1963), El Santo's son.

knownFor: One of Mexico's biggest masked draws, a feared main-eventer of CMLL and AAA.
reveal: Juan Manuel González Barrón (b. 1965), forced to unmask in 2017 and now wrestling as Rey Wagner.

knownFor: The masked high-flyer who became a global WWE superstar and one of the most famous luchadores ever.
reveal: Óscar Gutiérrez Rubio (b. 1974), unmasked once in WCW but re-masked for his entire WWE run.

knownFor: CMLL's beloved masked 'Atlantean prince,' one of lucha libre's greatest technical wrestlers.
reveal: A wrestler born in 1962 whose real name has never entered the public record.

knownFor: The original La Parka, whose full Day-of-the-Dead skeleton bodysuit and mask make him one of lucha's most striking figures.
reveal: Adolfo Margarito Tapia Ibarra (b. 1965).

portrait.author: Joshua W from The Americas, China

knownFor: The fierce Klingon second officer Klag in 'A Matter of Honor,' plus a Cardassian and Romulan Admiral Valdore.
reveal: The hulking square-jawed Klingon is genre heavy Brian Thompson, also known as the X-Files' Alien Bounty Hunter.

production: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine & The Next Generation
knownFor: Three different Ferengi—Gral, Prak and Bok—before later returning as a Malon and a Tellarite.
reveal: Buried under Ferengi lobes and teeth is Lee Arenberg, later famous as the pirate Pintel in the Pirates of the Caribbean films.

knownFor: L'Rell, the Klingon spy and warrior who rises to Chancellor of the Empire.
reveal: Utterly unrecognizable beneath Discovery's redesigned bald full-head Klingon prosthetics is American actress Mary Chieffo.

knownFor: Three different Klingons — Kol, Kol-Sha and Tenavik — across Discovery's first two seasons.
reveal: The heavy Klingon makeup hides Canadian actor Kenneth Mitchell, who kept acting after his 2018 ALS diagnosis and died in 2024.

knownFor: T'Kuvma, the messianic Klingon leader whose crusade ignites the Federation-Klingon war.
reveal: Under the elaborate albino-Klingon appliances is British-Nigerian actor Chris Obi, also Anubis on American Gods.

knownFor: Voq, the outcast albino Klingon surgically remade into human Starfleet officer Ash Tyler.
reveal: Voq was credited to fictitious actor 'Javid Iqbal' to hide the twist; the man under the makeup is British actor Shazad Latif.

knownFor: Hemmer, the blind telepathic Aenar (an Andorian offshoot) chief engineer of the Enterprise.
reveal: Beneath the blue skin, antennae and blank white eyes is Canadian actor and painter Bruce Horak, who is himself legally blind.

knownFor: The Keeper, lead Talosian in the original pilot 'The Cage' — the first-ever Star Trek villain.
reveal: The enlarged-brained, pulsing-veined Talosian was petite character actress Meg Wyllie, her voice dubbed by a male actor.

knownFor: The Klingon Captain Koloth, sparring with Kirk over tribbles and returning decades later on DS9.
reveal: Koloth (and the godlike alien Trelane) was 1950s Hollywood contract player William Campbell.

knownFor: A run of Star Trek aliens including the sloth-like Xindi-Arboreal councillor Jannar, a Kazon, a Borg drone and an android.
reveal: The full-face Xindi and Kazon prosthetics hide Rick Worthy, later a Cylon model on Battlestar Galactica.

knownFor: First Maje Jal Culluh, leader of the Kazon-Nistrim who menace Voyager in its early seasons.
reveal: Under the rocky Kazon skin and matted mane is Anthony De Longis, a whip-and-sword master and screen fight choreographer.

knownFor: Duras, the treacherous Klingon who murders K'Ehleyr and whose house drives years of Klingon intrigue.
reveal: The Klingon villain was an early acting role for Patrick Massett, who became a screenwriter (Friday Night Lights, Lara Croft: Tomb Raider).

knownFor: The rapidly-maturing 29th-century Borg drone 'One' on Voyager, plus a Vulcan, a Cardassian and a Nazi across the franchise.
reveal: The Borg implants and Vulcan ears belong to J. Paul Boehmer, a dependable Star Trek utility actor of the Berman era.

knownFor: The Vorta overseer Luaran, and earlier the Bajoran Minister Rozahn, on Deep Space Nine.
reveal: Behind the Vorta ears and pallor is actress Kitty Swink, wife of fellow Trek performer Armin Shimerman (Quark).

knownFor: Legate Tekeny Ghemor, the dissident Cardassian who comes to see Major Kira as a surrogate daughter.
reveal: The scaled Cardassian neck-ridges hide veteran stage and screen actor Lawrence Pressman.

knownFor: Gul Evek, the recurring Cardassian officer who clashes with the Maquis across three Star Trek series.
reveal: Evek's spoon-headed Cardassian makeup conceals American character actor Richard Poe, a Broadway and television regular.

production: Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
knownFor: Vixis, the Klingon first officer aboard Captain Klaa's Bird-of-Prey in Star Trek V.
reveal: The Klingon warrior-woman is stuntwoman, bodybuilder and actress Spice Williams-Crosby.

knownFor: Ikat'ika, the honorable Jem'Hadar First who duels Worf in the Dominion internment camp.
reveal: Under the horned, grey, scaled Jem'Hadar prosthetics is character actor James Horan, who also played Enterprise's shadowy 'Future Guy.'

knownFor: Sulan, the phage-ravaged Vidiian scientist who splits B'Elanna Torres into her human and Klingon halves in 'Faces.'
reveal: The gruesome patchwork Vidiian face belongs to Canadian-American actor Brian Markinson, who played the human Lt. Durst in the same episode.

knownFor: The first actor to play Davros, creator of the Daleks, originating the role in Genesis of the Daleks.
reveal: A prolific English character actor and sometime Dalek voice artist, his own face entirely swallowed by Friedlander's sunken-skulled mask; he reportedly rehearsed with a paper bag over his head to adjust to the restricted vision.

knownFor: The second actor to play Davros, in Destiny of the Daleks, wearing a hastily re-cast copy of the original mask.
reveal: An Indian-born British character actor, writer and former Cambridge Footlights member, unrecognisable inside the ill-fitting Davros appliance.

knownFor: The third and longest-serving classic-era Davros, across three 1980s serials and later Big Finish audios.
reveal: A busy radio actor far better known to millions as Mike Tucker in The Archers, buried under the Davros prosthetic on screen.

knownFor: A lead Dalek operator of the revived series, crouched inside the casing to drive the eyestalk, dome and gunstick.
reveal: A British actor, writer and prolific Big Finish audio director, completely sealed inside the Dalek shell with only his movements ever visible.

knownFor: A regular Dalek operator on the revived series and a Big Finish writer-director.
reveal: A British actor and director who, out of the casing, is a noted David Bowie authority and biographer.

knownFor: One of the two original Dalek voice artists, giving the creatures their grating ring-modulated snarl.

knownFor: Voices Endeavor in My Hero Academia, Sajin Komamura in Bleach and Ira Gamagoori in Kill la Kill.
reveal: The growl inside the blue Anubis-headed DekaMaster is Tetsu Inada — apparently Japan's go-to voice for noble dog-faced warriors, since he is also Bleach's wolf-headed captain Komamura.

knownFor: The thunderous voice of All Might in My Hero Academia.
reveal: That week's disposable Debo monster was voiced by Kenta Miyake — All Might himself, moonlighting on the wrong side of the heroes.

knownFor: One of Japan's busiest voice actors: Domon Kasshu in G Gundam, Gilgamesh in the Fate series and Doraemon's Suneo.

knownFor: The voice of Slayers' swordsman Gourry Gabriev and Fullmetal Alchemist's Jean Havoc.

reveal: Magiranger's first demon general — eighteen episodes of bellowing menace — was Hisao Egawa, who later spent years hidden inside Go-onger as the cheerful bus mecha Bus-on.

knownFor: Aoni Production character actor with three decades of Super Sentai and Kamen Rider monster voices.
reveal: The treacherous sorcerer-priest steering Magiranger's mid-season villainy was Yasuhiro Takato — a serial monster-of-the-week whose credits run from B-Fighter in 1995 to Kyoryuger and beyond.

knownFor: Aoni Production voice actor heard as Ultra-series aliens and thirty years of tokusatsu monsters.
reveal: From Gridman in 1993 to Kamen Rider in 2025, Yasuhiko Kawazu has been the unseen voice inside the rubber — including Abaranger's mushroom chimera Bankumushroom.

knownFor: The White Blood Cell U-1146 in Cells at Work! and Reiji Kizaki in World Trigger.
reveal: When the Netflix Ultraman anime needed its new Taro, the voice inside the suit was Tomoaki Maeno — anime's hardest-working white blood cell.

knownFor: A Theatre Echo mainstay for six decades and Japan's regular dub voice for Charlton Heston.
reveal: For forty years the dogged Interpol inspector forever one step behind Lupin III was Gorō Naya — a stage actor and director whose face the audience never saw.

knownFor: Producer of over 300 Power Rangers episodes who also voiced a legion of the show's monsters.
reveal: Divatox's wheezing, dome-shaped science officer Porto was the show's own supervising producer, Scott Page-Pagter, doing double duty in the dubbing booth.

knownFor: Gemini Award-winning Canadian puppeteer who apprenticed under Muppets legend Richard Hunt and worked Fraggle Rock.
reveal: Canada's beloved polar bear Basil was Tim Gosley for nine seasons — a Fraggle Rock alumnus operating the bear from below the frame.

knownFor: Jamal Turner on Netflix's On My Block.
reveal: The purple, mohawked wannabe-captain of the USS Protostar is On My Block's Jamal — Brett Gray, heard across the Delta Quadrant but never seen.

knownFor: The original Gordon on Sesame Street, and later a writer-producer on The Cosby Show.
reveal: Roosevelt Franklin, Sesame Street's scatting purple schoolroom star, was voiced by the show's own on-camera host: Matt Robinson, the first Gordon, moonlighting as a Muppet between his live scenes.

knownFor: Sesame Street's longtime songwriter and parodist (Put Down the Duckie, Born to Add), a Grammy and Emmy winner and son of Random House founder Bennett Cerf.
reveal: Little Chrissy, the piano-pounding Muppet rocker, is named for the man wailing inside him: Christopher Cerf, a Harvard-educated former Random House editor who sang his own Sesame Street rock parodies unseen, starting with Count It Higher in 1973.

production: The Return of the Living Dead
knownFor: Longtime Jim Henson Company puppeteer: the face of Fran Sinclair on Dinosaurs, Muppets Tonight's Fairyland PD chief, and the Puppet Up! improv troupe.
reveal: The Tarman — the dripping, brain-craving zombie that became one of horror's most iconic ghouls — was Allan Trautman, a physics graduate who spent the next three decades invisibly performing Muppets for the Henson company.

knownFor: Japanese voice veteran who giggled, roared and squealed as Dragon Ball's Majin Buu across three decades of series, films and games.
reveal: Every candy-craving gurgle of the pink djinn Majin Buu came from Kōzō Shioya, who first inflated into the role in 1994 and was still voicing Buu in games three decades later.

knownFor: Deep-voiced dean of unseen anime authority: King Bradley in Fullmetal Alchemist and narrator to generations of Japanese TV.
reveal: The Third Hokage's grandfatherly gravel belongs to Hidekatsu Shibata, a voice working invisibly since the 1960s — the same man who commanded as Führer King Bradley and menaced from inside dozens of tokusatsu monsters.

knownFor: One Piece mainstay who has voiced three different Straw Hat-adjacent characters: Jango, Mr. 2 Bon Clay, and the cyborg shipwright Franky.
reveal: The SUPER! cola-powered cyborg Franky is Kazuki Yao — a voice One Piece fans had already met twice without knowing it, as the hypnotist Jango and the ballerina assassin Bon Clay, before Franky made him permanent crew.

knownFor: Aoni Production stalwart heard in hundreds of anime and a long run of Super Sentai monsters-of-the-week.
reveal: Dragon Ball's leering shape-shifter pig Oolong — and Goku's monkey pal Bubbles — have both grunted with Naoki Tatsuta's voice since 1986, one of anime's great unseen utility players.

knownFor: Voice of Gundam Wing's Chang Wufei and Sengoku Basara's Chōsokabe Motochika.
reveal: Gundam Wing's severest pilot, the lone wolf Chang Wufei, is Ryuzou Ishino — thirty years of work, from Gundam cockpits to Sentai monster suits' voices, without audiences ever seeing his face.

knownFor: 81 Produce voice actor: One Piece's Dark King Silvers Rayleigh, Bobobo-bo Bo-bobo's Jelly Jiggler, and a string of Super Sentai monsters.
reveal: The Dark King Silvers Rayleigh — right hand of the Pirate King himself — speaks with the hidden voice of Keiichi Sonobe, a career monster-of-the-week man finally handed a legend.

knownFor: Japanese actor, narrator and voice actor: Bloodborne's Micolash, Berserk's Samson, and Sentai monsters from Megaranger to Timeranger.
reveal: When Naruto's puppet-master Sasori growls from inside his hunched battle-puppet Hiruko, that is Yutaka Aoyama — a voice actor hiding inside a character who is himself hiding inside a puppet.

knownFor: Prolific anime voice actor best known as Shinpachi Shimura in Gintama and Uso Ewin in Mobile Suit Victory Gundam.
reveal: The fortune-slip monster menacing the Zenkaigers is voiced by Gintama's eternal straight man — Shinpachi himself, moonlighting unseen as a Sentai monster of the week.

knownFor: Veteran Japanese voice actor remembered as Anakaris, Bishamon, Sasquatch and Victor von Gerdenheim in the Darkstalkers games.
reveal: For decades the roars of Super Sentai's monsters-of-the-week — Zyuranger's Dora Antaeus among them — poured out of this Darkstalkers stalwart, who never once appeared on screen.

knownFor: Hikaru Sulu, helmsman of the USS Enterprise in Star Trek.
reveal: Years before he sat at the Enterprise helm, a young George Takei anonymously dubbed English dialogue for Rodan and Godzilla Raids Again — Sulu was in kaiju movies first.

knownFor: Stage-trained character actor with Broadway Shakespeare repertory and five Shakespeare in the Park productions behind him.
reveal: Trek buried him three times over: as the Klingon Kulge (killed by Gowron), as the Bajoran leader Woban, and finally under the full pig-snouted Tellarite prosthetic of bounty hunter Skalaar.

knownFor: Stage actor who originated Scar in Broadway's The Lion King.
reveal: Under the Minbari warrior's bone crest is the man who first wore Scar's mask on Broadway — and he kept vanishing in Trek latex too, as the Cardassian Rusot and a Klingon prosecutor.

knownFor: Seven seasons as Tuvok, Voyager's Vulcan security chief.
reveal: Before Voyager, Russ was already buried in Trek latex as the Klingon mercenary T'Kar; then came seven years of pointed ears, plus Tuvok's mirror double and a Changeling impostor.

portrait.author: Super Festivals from Ft. Lauderdale, USA

knownFor: Craggy American character actor of Scarface, Ghostbusters II and over a hundred screen roles.
reveal: Under the grey Cardassian shell in DS9's 'Duet' — often called the series' finest hour — is Harris Yulin, playing a haunted file clerk impersonating the war criminal Gul Darhe'el: a disguise inside a disguise.

knownFor: Half a century as Star Trek's half-Vulcan science officer.
reveal: The ears were glued on fresh by makeup artist Fred Phillips every morning of the run. Nimoy spent years wrestling with the pointed shadow they cast, titling his memoirs I Am Not Spock — and then I Am Spock.

knownFor: Ubiquitous American character actor with more than half a dozen Star Trek roles across the franchise.
reveal: Kopache is Trek's quiet quick-change artist: the Romulan Mirok, the Vulcan Tos, and — most movingly — Kira's dying Bajoran father Taban, a new forehead nearly every time he clocked in.

knownFor: British-Canadian classical actor and former associate director of the Stratford Festival.
reveal: The treacherous Romulan admiral who outplays Data in 'Data's Day' was a Shakespearean director under the ear tips — Scarfe returned to the latex chair as the Romulan Tokath and Voyager's Mokra commandant Augris.

portrait.author: Jeff Hitchcock from Vancouver, BC, Canada

knownFor: Moira Queen on Arrow and Karen Sammler on Once and Again.
reveal: Arrow's poised Moira Queen once ruled a very different collective: Thompson wore the Borg Queen's cabled skull and pallid biomechanical skin for Voyager, having already hidden in Trek as a Romulan and a Trill.

knownFor: Comedian and podcast mainstay who voices Mr. Peanutbutter on BoJack Horseman.
reveal: The Cerritos' bird-like ship's counselor is Mr. Peanutbutter himself — Tompkins never shows his face in the gig, but the ebullient cadence is unmistakable once you know.

knownFor: Leeta the Bajoran dabo girl on Star Trek: Deep Space Nine.
reveal: Five seasons behind Bajoran nose ridges: Masterson's dabo girl married a Ferengi, channeled the Trill host Emony Dax in a zhian'tara ritual, and turned mirror-universe rebel.

portrait.author: Permission from Chase Masterson Official Management

production: Sesame Street Presents: Follow That Bird
knownFor: Canadian puppeteer and Emmy-winning children's television writer who created The Big Comfy Couch.
reveal: Inside the giant, meddling bird social worker who chases Big Bird across America is Cheryl Wagner, who spent Fraggle Rock's run as Jim Henson's right hand and the voice of Ma Gorg. A decade later she would invent The Big Comfy Couch.

knownFor: Obie- and Emmy-winning New York actress and cabaret comedian seen in Foul Play and Can't Stop the Music.
reveal: Ma Otter's gentle voice and songs belong to Marilyn Sokol, a Bronx-born comedian who began her career as a belly dancer in Man of La Mancha — heard but never seen while the puppet worked the river on camera.

knownFor: Emmy-winning comedy writer for Sesame Street, The Electric Company and Square One TV who scripted gags for Bob Hope, Carol Burnett and Bob Newhart.
reveal: The tiny hero doodled on a kitchen cup speaks with the voice of Jim Thurman, the veteran gag man who co-created the segment with animator Paul Fierlinger — a writer who spent four decades hidden inside Sesame Street's scripts and soundtracks.

knownFor: Sesame Street and Muppets puppeteer whose credits run from Between the Lions to Crank Yankers and Walking with Dinosaurs.
reveal: The man-eating plant swallowing Skid Row whole eight shows a week concealed Paul McGinnis, a Sesame Street Muppet performer sweating inside the full-grown pod while the plant's singing voice came from offstage.

knownFor: Doll artist and puppet maker called "the mother of Yoda," who sculpted the gelflings Jen and Kira for The Dark Crystal.
reveal: She formed Yoda's body from foam, modeled his hands and feet — and then stayed to assist Frank Oz in puppeteering him, an unseen extra pair of hands on the Dagobah set.

knownFor: Japanese actress and voice actress known as Rikka Takarada in SSSS.Gridman.
reveal: Zenkaiger's female lead never shows a human face: Magine is a robot Kikainoid, a suit performed by a suit actor while Yume Miyamoto — Gridman's Rikka — plays the series' heroine entirely in voice.

knownFor: Deep-voiced Japanese villain specialist best known as Sir Crocodile in One Piece.
reveal: Timeranger's crime-boss alien, running rackets across the year 2000 for 47 episodes, is Ryūzaburō Ōtomo — the booming bass who is also One Piece's Crocodile.

knownFor: Japanese voice actor best known as Goro Majima in the Yakuza video game series.
reveal: The monster-composing villain who conducts Abaranger's Trinoids for 48 episodes sings with the voice of Hidenari Ugaki — Goro Majima, the Mad Dog of Shimano himself.

knownFor: Japanese voice actor best known as Neji Hyuga in Naruto.
reveal: The wisecracking paper lantern who fights beside the Shinkengers is Kōichi Tōchika — the voice of Naruto's stoic prodigy Neji Hyuga, moonlighting as sentai's chattiest light fixture.

knownFor: Prolific 81 Produce voice actor who voiced monsters across more than a dozen Super Sentai series before ALS ended his career in 2023.
reveal: The fawning crocodile Trinoid who refused to die and hung around for thirty-odd episodes was Kyōsei Tsukui — to a generation of Japanese children, the beloved voice of NHK's cat mascot Nyanchuu.

knownFor: Japanese voice actor known for Lloyd Asplund in Code Geass and Gluttony in Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.
reveal: Under the thieving Deboth monster's voice is Tetsu Shiratori, Code Geass's giddy engineer Lloyd Asplund — one of the anime regulars who quietly staff sentai's monster-of-the-week rotation.

production: Star Trek: The Next Generation "Chain of Command"
knownFor: British screen-villain royalty: The Omen's photographer Jennings, Tron's Sark, Titanic's Lovejoy.
reveal: The Cardassian torturer who insisted there were five lights was David Warner — fresh off dying as Klingon chancellor Gorkon in Star Trek VI, he vanished into a second alien face within a year.

production: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine "Apocalypse Rising"
knownFor: Chief Miles O'Brien across twelve seasons of TNG and DS9, and Ireland's everyman of The Commitments.
reveal: The Klingon 'Pahash' of the House of Konjah is Chief O'Brien himself — for one undercover mission, Trek's most stubbornly human character disappeared into the full ridges and armor.

production: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine "Rocks and Shoals"
knownFor: Seinfeld's fast-talking attorney Jackie Chiles.
reveal: The Jem'Hadar soldier who marches knowingly to his death for the Dominion's honor is Jackie Chiles — Morris traded courtroom patter for horns, scales and one of DS9's most quietly devastating performances.

portrait.author: pinguino k from North Hollywood, USA

knownFor: Hollywood's go-to exasperated dad: Breaking Away, Sixteen Candles, Popeye's Wimpy.
reveal: The retired head of the Obsidian Order — the most quietly terrifying Cardassian ever written — is the same Paul Dooley who forgot Molly Ringwald's birthday.

knownFor: Norman Wilson in The Wire and an Emmy-winning turn as rib-joint sage Freddy in House of Cards.
reveal: The Klingon commander menacing relay station 47 is Reg E. Cathey — that unmistakable subterranean voice later served barbecue to Frank Underwood.

knownFor: Captain Rachel Garrett, doomed commander of the Enterprise-C in "Yesterday's Enterprise".
reveal: The Enterprise-C's heroic captain kept sneaking back into the franchise wearing new faces — as prickly Klingon warp scientist Kurak, then twice more as the Obsidian Order's watcher Korinas.

knownFor: Smartgunner Drake in Aliens and Bogs Diamond in The Shawshank Redemption.
reveal: The Klingon captain hounding the NX-01 through the Borderland is Drake from Aliens — Rolston swapped his smartgun harness for cranial ridges and a bird-of-prey.

production: Star Trek: Voyager "Message in a Bottle"
knownFor: Khan's blond lieutenant Joachim in Star Trek II — a role he famously went uncredited for.
reveal: Sixteen years after standing uncredited at Khan's right hand, Scott slipped back into the franchise under Romulan ears as Commander Rekar, hijacker of the USS Prometheus.

knownFor: Prolific character and voice actor who played a different alien in three different Star Trek series.
reveal: Swetow opened Deep Space Nine's pilot as the sabre-rattling Cardassian Gul Jasad, then returned years later in blue skin and antennae as Andorian ambassador Thoris — never once with his own face.

production: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine "Civil Defense"
knownFor: Veteran Chicago stage and screen character actor.
reveal: Goldring played five different aliens across three Star Trek series without ever showing his own face — Cardassian Legate Kell, the Nazi-uniformed Hirogen hunter Karr of "The Killing Game", and a Nausicaan pirate captain among them.

knownFor: Played the title serial killer in the 2002 biopic Ted Bundy.
reveal: Before he was Ted Bundy — and before three episodes in Vulcan ears as T'Pol's fiancé Koss — Burke was wired into full Borg prosthetics as Goval, one of Lore's liberated drones.

knownFor: Voyager's defining traitor, plus the Romulan officer T'Rul in DS9's "The Search".
reveal: Hackett wore two disguises stacked on top of each other: a Cardassian agent surgically hidden inside a Bajoran face — the audience, like the Maquis, never saw her coming.

knownFor: Dancer turned actress — a founding member of the Pussycat Dolls.
reveal: The first face of Gul Dukat's gentle half-Cardassian daughter belonged to a founding Pussycat Doll — Batten later went green all over as the Orion seductress Navaar in Enterprise's "Bound".

production: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
knownFor: Stepped into Kirstie Alley's role as the Vulcan Saavik for two feature films.
reveal: Recast mid-franchise, Curtis disappeared so completely into Saavik's ears and Vulcan reserve that many viewers never clocked the swap — she later doubled the trick as the Vulcan spy posing as Romulan mercenary Tallera in TNG's "Gambit".

knownFor: Detective Matthew Sikes, the human half of Alien Nation's odd couple.
reveal: After playing the human partner opposite an alien for all of Alien Nation, Graham crossed to the other side of the makeup chair and spent Enterprise's entire run under the ears and brows of Vulcan ambassador Soval.

portrait.author: Keith McDuffee from Northborough, MA, USA

knownFor: The Great Escape and a run of 1960s guest villainy; he was contracted as the standby replacement for Leonard Nimoy's Spock.
reveal: Stonn, the smug Vulcan waiting to claim Spock's bride, was played by the man the studio had lined up to replace Nimoy himself — Montaigne had already flown for the other side as Romulan officer Decius in "Balance of Terror".

knownFor: Captain Liam Shaw, the abrasive fan-favorite commander of the Titan in Picard season 3.
reveal: Two decades before Captain Shaw was ordering Picard off his bridge, Stashwick was already in the franchise — invisible under the pointed ears of Talok, a Romulan mole passing as a Vulcan.

knownFor: Irish stage and screen mainstay — Lydia in Into the Badlands.
reveal: Picard's Romulan housekeeper-turned-love-interest is one of Ireland's most acclaimed actresses, working through brow ridges and pointed ears for all three seasons — plus a second hidden turn as the Supervisor Tallinn.

portrait.author: Virgin Media Dublin International Film Festival

knownFor: Kumiko in The Karate Kid Part II and Waverly in The Joy Luck Club.
reveal: Starfleet's treacherous security chief, her ears sharpened into Romulan points behind Zhat Vash sunglasses, is Karate Kid royalty hiding a beloved 1980s face in plain sight.

knownFor: Garrett McNeill on six seasons of NBC's Superstore.
reveal: The scheming Klingon captain of the Che'Ta' is Garrett from Superstore — Dunn went from the Cloud 9 intercom to a warship's command chair without ever showing his face.

production: Star Trek: The Animated Series "The Time Trap"
knownFor: Uhura herself — Star Trek's pioneering communications officer.
reveal: While Uhura sat at her station on screen, Nichols moonlighted invisibly as the animated series' other women — including Devna, the Orion Interpreter of Laws ruling over the Delta Triangle.

portrait.author: Photo by Desilu Productions-release sent by Cooper & Colin, Chicago--apparently a publicity agency for Ebony magazine.

knownFor: Emmy-winning current official voice of Bugs Bunny, Daffy Duck, Tweety and Marvin the Martian.
reveal: The throat behind Lower Decks' assorted Ferengi bit players belongs to the reigning Bugs Bunny — Bauza carries virtually all of Mel Blanc's menagerie, and you will never see his face doing it.

knownFor: Seven-time Academy Award-winning makeup effects master behind An American Werewolf in London, Harry and the Hendersons, and Men in Black.
reveal: The giant ape terrorizing 1976 New York is the makeup legend himself — hired to build the suit when the full-size mechanical Kong flopped, Baker ended up wearing his own creation for virtually every shot of the monster.

knownFor: Prolific American voice actress heard across cartoons and games, from Young Justice's Black Canary to a string of unseen Star Trek roles.
reveal: The Ghost's steady Twi'lek captain is a Princeton- and NYU Tisch-trained actress who was discovered playing fifteen characters in a one-woman show — and has never needed her face on screen since.

portrait.author: Super Festivals from Ft. Lauderdale, USA

knownFor: Canadian voice actor who was Miroku in InuYasha, Goku in Ocean's Dragon Ball Z dub, Cyclops in X-Men: Evolution, and Cole in LEGO Ninjago.
reveal: The roguish monk's voice belonged to a comedian from Jasper, Alberta who anchored two decades of Vancouver dubbing — the only place fans ever saw his actual face was as Captain Kleinman on Stargate Atlantis.

knownFor: Veteran Aoni Production voice actor and narrator known for Patlabor's trigger-happy Isao Ohta, Urusei Yatsura's Onsen-Mark, and a parade of Showa-era Kamen Rider monsters.
reveal: The voices snarling out of Showa Kamen Rider's rubber-suited kaijin and Patlabor's hotheaded cop all trace back to one Waseda-educated narrator, honored with a Merit Award at the 2022 Seiyu Awards after six unseen decades.

knownFor: Golden-age American radio star of The First Nighter and The Falcon who later played General Mann in The War of the Worlds (1953).
reveal: The uncredited voice narrating and dubbing the American cut of King Kong vs. Godzilla was one of radio's most famous men — a star once ranked among the best-known voices in America alongside Bing Crosby.

knownFor: One of Japan's busiest voice actors, defined by the deadpan baritone he gave Gintama's silver-haired slacker samurai.
reveal: Gintoki's lazy drawl belongs to Tomokazu Sugita, a mic-booth chameleon whose voice also threads unseen through kaiju and tokusatsu rosters. Two decades of hearing him, never once seeing him.

knownFor: Award-winning seiyu behind Kaworu Nagisa in Neon Genesis Evangelion and Athrun Zala in Gundam SEED, voted Japan's most popular voice actor in 2004.
reveal: The Wicked Dragon Dembey snarling through GoGo Sentai Boukenger and Naruto's haunted sand-wielder Gaara are the same invisible man: Akira Ishida, the gentle voice that made Evangelion's Kaworu unforgettable.

knownFor: Veteran Aoni Production voice actor and narrator, long heard as Nobita's schoolteacher in Doraemon and Cancer Deathmask in Saint Seiya.
reveal: Devilman's demon-hero growl came from a young Ryōichi Tanaka, who then spent half a century as one of anime and tokusatsu's most invisible men — the census caught him lending voices to kaiju-era monsters too.

knownFor: Played Susan Robinson, one of Sesame Street's original human neighbors, from 1969 to 2016.
reveal: It's Susan. While Loretta Long played Sesame Street's warmest on-camera face, she slipped off-screen to voice the proud, prodding mother of Muppet schoolkid Roosevelt Franklin — plus talk-show diva Suzetta Something.

knownFor: Canadian comedian whose one-woman show My Boyfriend's Back and There's Gonna Be Laundry made her a solo-theatre star and Order of Ontario appointee.
reveal: Years before Canadian theatres knew her name, Sandra Shamas crouched out of frame on Fraggle Rock, bringing a magic mirror and a squad of tiny Doozers to life.

production: The Dark Crystal: Age of Resistance
designer: Brian Froud / Jim Henson's Creature Shop
knownFor: Jim Henson Company mainstay who anchors the Puppet Up! improv troupe, performed Waldorf in From the Balcony, and puppeteered on Crank Yankers and Greg the Bunny.
reveal: Hup, the spoon-brandishing Podling who dreams of becoming a Paladin, is Victor Yerrid — one of the few Age of Resistance performers who both moved his creature and gave it his own voice.

knownFor: Voice of Itachi Uchiha in Naruto and Auron in Final Fantasy X.
reveal: The whirring duke of the Org horde speaks with the same voice that made Itachi Uchiha an icon. Hideo Ishikawa has haunted tokusatsu for decades, from Gaoranger's Propela to Kyuranger's Gamettsui.

knownFor: Voice of the fox familiar Tomoe in Kamisama Kiss.
reveal: The monster-of-the-week Gakkarize is voiced by the man romance-anime fans know as Tomoe. Shinnosuke Tachibana later returned to the franchise as Dr. Iokaru in the Zenkaiger V-Cinema.

knownFor: Veteran Aoni Production voice actor and narrator active since 1988.
reveal: The nunchaku-swinging Barbaric Machine Beast is Takahiro Yoshimizu, a thirty-year Aoni veteran who keeps slipping back into Sentai monster suits by voice alone, from Timeranger's Arms Smuggler Hammer to Kyoryuger's Debo specials.

knownFor: Played Red Racer in Gekisou Sentai Carranger and voices Ken Masters in Street Fighter.
reveal: Five years after leading Carranger as Red Racer with his own face, Yūji Kishi came back to Sentai on the other side of the camera — literally, as the monster Camera Org. He is also the long-running Japanese voice of Street Fighter's Ken Masters.

knownFor: Voice of Buffaloman in Kinnikuman and the series villain Dezumozorlya in Abaranger.
reveal: The parasitic god that drives all of Abaranger's evil is Masaharu Satō, the gravel voice behind Kinnikuman's Buffaloman — a man who has voiced monsters in nearly every Sentai era without once showing his face.

knownFor: Voiced Kozo Fuyutsuki in Neon Genesis Evangelion and Walter in Hellsing.
reveal: Evangelion's gentle Vice Commander Fuyutsuki spent the 1970s voicing masked villains in the very first Super Sentai. Hideaki Anno cast Motomu Kiyokawa specifically because he loved his Gorenger work.

knownFor: Voice of Heero Yuy in Mobile Suit Gundam Wing.
reveal: The dragon king who torments GoGoFive for half a season is Heero Yuy himself — Hikaru Midorikawa, one of anime's biggest leading men, moonlighting inside Sentai's rubber-suited demons from Timeranger to Ryusoulger.

knownFor: Voiced Ganondorf in The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess.
reveal: The ninja spirit Furaimaru — and Gaoranger's Magic Flute Org, and Abaranger's fishing-mad Trinoid — is Hironori Miyata, the voice Nintendo chose for Ganondorf. He reprised Furaimaru a decade later in Gokaiger.

knownFor: Voice actor and sound director who voiced Ultraman Ace in Ultraman: Super Fighter Legend.
reveal: Behind Ohranger's Cat Signal is Tomohiro Nishimura, a voice actor turned sound director who has also spoken for Ultraman Ace — and who is perpetually mistaken for the similarly named Tomomichi Nishimura.

knownFor: Voice of Kazuma Sato in KonoSuba.
reveal: The duplicating menace Copy World is KonoSuba's Kazuma — Jun Fukushima, cast as a monster whose whole gimmick is being a knockoff. He voiced both the World and its giant Dai form.

knownFor: Voice of the Producer in The Idolmaster Cinderella Girls; Seiyu Awards Best Rookie winner.
reveal: The film-headed monster hijacking the Kiramager crossover movie is Shunsuke Takeuchi, whose impossibly deep baritone made the Idolmaster Producer famous before he was twenty.

knownFor: Voice actor who debuted as Soichiro Arima in His and Her Circumstances.
reveal: Zenkaiger's dried-persimmon monster is Chihiro Suzuki, the romantic lead of Kare Kano, cheerfully buried inside a snack-themed machine beast and its skyscraper-sized Dai form.

knownFor: Voice of Toma Kamijo in A Certain Magical Index.
reveal: The rice-cake monster of Zenkaiger episode 10 is Toma Kamijo's voice, Atsushi Abe — who even returned to be re-mulched into Kikai Kashiwamochi World for the Donbrothers crossover.

knownFor: Anime voice actor with Mausu Promotion.
reveal: The daylight-hoarding Mahiru World, who trapped Zenkaiger's city in permanent high noon, is anime leading man Atsushi Tamaru, unrecognizable inside a walking sundial of a monster.

knownFor: Voice of Yuki Sohma in Fruits Basket.
reveal: Zenkaiger's love-crazed Renai World, who set the whole cast swooning, is voiced by Ryōta Ōsaka — the actual romance-anime prince behind Fruits Basket's Yuki Sohma.

knownFor: Osaka-born voice actor affiliated with Stay Luck.
reveal: The monster that turned Zenkaiger's world into weekly shonen manga panels is Yukihiro Nozuyama, a young seiyuu whose face never appears — only his ink-slinging alter ego and its giant Dai form.

portrait.author: numan ch-ヌーマンチャンネル-【公式】

knownFor: Voice of Takemichi Hanagaki in Tokyo Revengers.
reveal: Tokyo Revengers' crybaby hero Takemichi is the voice inside Zenkaiger's milk monster, Gyunyu World. Yuuki Shin poured himself into a walking dairy nightmare and its giant form.

knownFor: Voice of Jonathan Joestar in JoJo's Bizarre Adventure.
reveal: The topsy-turvy Sakasama World, who flipped Zenkaiger's city upside down, speaks with the noble voice of Jonathan Joestar — Kazuyuki Okitsu, gleefully inverted.

knownFor: Ken Production voice actor heard as Tecchi in Sword Art Online.
reveal: Zenkaiger's noodle-obsessed Men World is Nobuyuki Kobushi, a character-actor seiyuu who slurped his way through both the monster and its giant Dai form without ever being seen.

knownFor: Voice of Shin Ichijo in the King of Prism series.
reveal: The monster that forced all of Zenkaiger's city into a deadly game of tag is King of Prism idol Junta Terashima — a sparkling stage prince recast as a playground demon.

knownFor: Seiyu Awards Best Rookie winner affiliated with VIMS.
reveal: The vacation-brainwashing Vacances World — a monster so relaxing it lulled its own army — is award-winning young seiyuu Taku Yashiro on an unseen working holiday.

knownFor: Veteran actor and voice actor with Production Taiku.
reveal: Zenkaiger's tyrannical school monster, who trapped heroes in an eternal classroom, is Jin Urayama — a stage veteran in his sixties conducting the lesson entirely by voice.

knownFor: Voice of Madara Uchiha in Naruto Shippuden.
reveal: The monster that shrank Zenkaiger's cast into super-deformed chibis is voiced by Madara Uchiha himself — Naoya Uchida, one of anime's most feared villain voices, playing it for laughs.

knownFor: Voiced Ryunosuke's father in Urusei Yatsura.
reveal: Changeman's Dodon is Masahiro Anzai, the booming comic voice of Ryunosuke's father in Urusei Yatsura, who also growled through Kakuranger's yokai before illness ended his career in 1997.

knownFor: Voice of Broly in the Dragon Ball Z films.
reveal: Hurricaneger's pun-spewing masked general Satorakura is the Legendary Super Saiyan — Bin Shimada, Broly's original voice, who has also spoken for Gaoranger's Tire Org and a menagerie of Ultra monsters.

knownFor: Voice of the protagonist in The Tatami Galaxy and Takatoshi Tsuda in Seitokai Yakuindomo.
reveal: Zenkaiger's hot-headed robot ranger Juran — a full-time hero who is never human on screen — is Shintarō Asanuma, the motor-mouthed star of The Tatami Galaxy, present in all 49 episodes by voice alone.

knownFor: Voice of Alucard in Hellsing and Kirei Kotomine in the Fate franchise.
reveal: Zenkaiger's throne-bound tyrant Bokkowaus is Alucard and Kirei Kotomine in one — Jouji Nakata, who began in Sentai on camera as Liveman's Great Professor Bias and now rules it unseen.

knownFor: Voice of Iori Yagami in The King of Fighters.
reveal: The dark ranger Nezi Black of Megaranger's evil Nejiranger team is Kunihiko Yasui — the voice of King of Fighters' Iori Yagami — who kept coming back as Gekiranger's snake-fist Braco and Shinkenger's Ayakashi Gozunagumo.

knownFor: Japan Action Enterprise stuntwoman-turned-actress who played Yoko Minato in Kamen Rider Gaim.
reveal: Nearly every Kamen Rider hands the armor to a stunt double after the henshin — but producer Naomi Takebe asked Tsukui, a trained JAE suit actress, to do both. She played Yoko Minato barefaced and then vanished into the Marika suit to fight as herself.

knownFor: Award-winning Japanese leading man of dramas like Quartet and MIU404, and Shin Godzilla's ministry officer Yasuda.
reveal: Shin Ultraman kept the giant's serene voice a secret; only a month after release did Tsuburaya confirm it was Issey Takahashi — the drama star who had already faced a Shin-universe kaiju on foot in Shin Godzilla.

knownFor: Prolific Japanese voice actor who later voiced Kintaros and Den-O Ax Form in Kamen Rider Den-O.
reveal: Kamen Rider Black's silver nemesis spoke with the voice of stage actor Masaki Terasoma — his very first voice role, delivered while a suit actor wore the armor. Two decades later he was back in the franchise as the bear Imagin Kintaros.

production: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
knownFor: Legendary stage and screen actor — Captain von Trapp in The Sound of Music and an Oscar winner for Beginners.
reveal: The Shakespeare-quoting Klingon is Christopher Plummer, who talked designer Richard Snell out of the wig and most of the ridges — leaving a bald warrior with an eyepatch bolted to his skull and a face that is unmistakably Plummer's.

knownFor: Twice-Oscar-nominated star of Sideways, John Adams, and The Holdovers.
reveal: The part-Klingon, part-Tellarite villain is lifelong Trekkie Paul Giamatti. Alex Kurtzman offered him his pick of roles after watching him gush about Star Trek on the Holdovers press tour — and Giamatti chose the one buried under alien prosthetics.

knownFor: Star Trek's longtime stunt coordinator across TNG, DS9, and Voyager, and singer of the post-9/11 anthem 'America We Stand as One'.
reveal: The Klingon Intelligence operative skulking around the station is the man who choreographed the punches: stunt coordinator Dennis 'Danger' Madalone, who slipped under Westmore's prosthetics whenever a scene needed a body he could trust — his own.

knownFor: Ubiquitous character actor — Kasuf in Stargate, the museum curator in The Mummy, and a regular alien-of-the-week across genre TV.
reveal: The evasive Klingon adjutant who stonewalls Picard's request for a cloaked ship in 'Unification' is Erick Avari, one of Hollywood's most recognizable that-guy faces, here erased under a full Westmore Klingon headpiece.

knownFor: Yale Drama-trained actor best known barefaced as Bodie Wells on Dawson's Creek.
reveal: Martok's swaggering son — the Klingon lout Worf thrashes to get the general's attention in 'The Way of the Warrior' — was Obi Ndefo, unrecognizable under the ridges years before Dawson's Creek made his real face familiar.

production: Ultra Galaxy Fight: The Absolute Conspiracy
knownFor: American martial artist and actor, famous in Japan as Kakuranger's Ninja Black and as a Sasuke/Ninja Warrior standout.
reveal: Twenty-five years after playing Ultraman Powered's human host Kenichi Kai, Kane Kosugi returned to the character completely unseen — voicing the giant himself in the English version of Ultra Galaxy Fight.

knownFor: Former Super Sentai heroine — Yoko Usami/Yellow Buster in Tokumei Sentai Go-Busters — who became one of anime's busiest voice actresses.
reveal: Behind Aqours' strict student-council president Dia Kurosawa is Arisa Komiya, the same performer who spent 2012 fighting monsters on camera as Go-Busters' Yellow Buster before disappearing entirely into voice work.

knownFor: Panamanian stage-trained character actor best known as bus passenger Ortiz in Speed.
reveal: The scheming Klingon who attacked the House of Kozak with accountants instead of bat'leths — and was discommendated by Gowron for raising a blade against an unarmed Ferengi — is Speed's Ortiz.

knownFor: Best known as Howard "Bunny" Colvin, the district major who legalized drugs in Hamsterdam on The Wire.
reveal: Under the Klingon defector-ambassador's ridges in the war-crimes episode "Under the Cloak of War" is The Wire's Bunny Colvin, trading Baltimore realpolitik for J'Gal's ghosts.

knownFor: Character and voice actor whose Wikipedia entry leads with one credit: the voice of Skyrim's dragon apocalypse.
reveal: Alduin the World-Eater, the black dragon that opens Skyrim, is Daniel Riordan — the same actor who glowered under Duras's Klingon prosthetics while hunting Captain Archer across Star Trek: Enterprise.

knownFor: Soap opera royalty as India von Halkein on Guiding Light.
reveal: Under the spotted Narn headpiece of G'Kar's aide Na'Toth is a CBS soap baroness — who then crossed franchises to become Grilka, the Klingon widow who married Quark on Deep Space Nine.

knownFor: Former NFL player turned actor, best known as the sword-wielding Nathaniel Moon in Into the Badlands.
reveal: The dead Klingon warrior who presses a branding iron to B'Elanna's cheek on the Barge of the Dead is an ex-NFL pro who later became Into the Badlands' deadliest regent.

knownFor: Stand-up comedian and longtime senior correspondent on The Daily Show.
reveal: That voice in Lower Decks' Klingon honor-politics episode "A Farewell to Farms" — Enaj, plus Ma'ah and Malor's mother — is The Daily Show's Dulcé Sloan, never once showing her face.

portrait.author: LBJ Library photo by Jay Godwin

knownFor: Cult-horror mainstay who played Otis in Henry: Portrait of a Serial Killer and appeared in Rob Zombie's films.
reveal: The dying Klingon first officer whose telepathic parasites hijack the DS9 crew in "Dramatis Personae" is Otis from Henry: Portrait of a Serial Killer — one of horror's most unnerving faces, fully buried in ridges.

knownFor: Immortalized as Emperor Wang the Perverted in the cult spoof Flesh Gordon.
reveal: The old Klingon ally of the House of Mogh who shares bloodwine and battle songs with Worf in "The Way of the Warrior" spent the 1970s as Flesh Gordon's leering Emperor Wang — a career spent under designed faces.

knownFor: Stage director and actor who ran the Oregon Shakespeare Festival as artistic director from 1991 to 1995.
reveal: Inside the full saurian prosthetics of Gegen, the Voth paleontologist persecuted for proving his people evolved from Earth's dinosaurs, is a former artistic director of the Oregon Shakespeare Festival — who also did Klingon duty as J'Dan in TNG's "The Drumhead".

knownFor: Best known as the recurring Lt. Bob Brooks on Matlock.
reveal: The Klingon commander who courteously offers Worf a place in the Defense Force at the end of "Heart of Glory" is Matlock's Lt. Brooks under a forehead of Westmore rubber.

knownFor: Actor and producer who founded Los Angeles' Ebony Repertory Theatre.
reveal: The Klingon patriarch of a generational ship who decides B'Elanna's unborn daughter is his people's messiah is the founder of LA's Ebony Repertory Theatre.

production: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
knownFor: Doc Brown of the Back to the Future trilogy and Taxi's Reverend Jim.
reveal: One year before he fired up the DeLorean, Christopher Lloyd was Commander Kruge — the Klingon whose kill order cost Kirk his son and who grappled with him over the lava of the dying Genesis planet.

knownFor: The A-Team's relentless Colonel Decker, after years as Elvis Presley's stunt and body double.
reveal: The gravel-voiced Klingon captain of the sleeper ship T'Ong, waking up still at war with the Federation, is Colonel Decker — the man the A-Team could never shake.

knownFor: Character-actor legend: NewsRadio's Jimmy James, Office Space's Milton, and Barry's Fuches.
reveal: The Klingon captain who grudgingly smuggles Picard and Data to Romulus in "Unification" is Milton from Office Space. He'd burn the ship down for that stapler.

knownFor: Beloved as Uncle Phil on The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air and the voice of Shredder in the 1987 Ninja Turtles cartoon.
reveal: Uncle Phil is under there: James Avery played General K'Vagh in the Enterprise two-parter that finally explained why 1960s Klingons had smooth foreheads.

knownFor: Friends' mustachioed building superintendent Mr. Treeger.
reveal: The Duras-loyal Klingon captain trying to blast Kurn out of the sky in the "Redemption" civil war is Mr. Treeger from Friends.

knownFor: Seinfeld's Mr. Wilhelm and Voyager's Admiral Owen Paris, with a genre pedigree going back to V.
reveal: Trek fans know Richard Herd's bare face as Tom Paris's father — but in "Birthright" he vanished under Klingon prosthetics as L'Kor, the elder living out an honorless exile in a Romulan prison camp.

knownFor: Moke the roadie in This Is Spinal Tap and Johnny Horne in Twin Peaks.
reveal: The wounded renegade over whom television's first Klingon death ritual was performed — eyes pried open, warriors howling at the sky — is Spinal Tap's roadie Moke.

production: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
knownFor: Won four consecutive Emmys as Night Court's sleazy prosecutor Dan Fielding.
reveal: Kruge's last surviving officer — the Klingon Kirk tells "I lied" about killing — is Night Court's Dan Fielding under the ridges.

knownFor: Actor turned writer-producer who co-created Godfather of Harlem.
reveal: Every one of Eckstein's Star Trek appearances was in mask and heavy makeup — two Jem'Hadar, two Hirogen, and this Klingon mutineer — before he took off the rubber for good and co-created Godfather of Harlem.

knownFor: RoboCop's Sergeant Reed and Robert Altman's Nashville.
reveal: The kindly Klingon who gives the mind-wiped Kurn a new identity in "Sons of Mogh" is the desk sergeant who ran RoboCop's precinct.

knownFor: Stage veteran recognizable as the doomed Anna Morgan in The Ring.
reveal: Martok's imperious wife, who puts Jadzia through the trials of the House of Martok, is The Ring's cursed Anna Morgan — and she went on to assassinate the entire Romulan Senate as Tal'aura in Nemesis.

knownFor: Child actor who was the first of many to play Worf's son, later a punk-rock frontman.
reveal: The very first Alexander — the little Klingon who meets his father Worf in "Reunion" at age six — grew up to front the Portland punk band P.R.O.B.L.E.M.S.

knownFor: Mickey Mouse Club alum who voiced Tony Stark in the animated Ultimate Avengers films.
reveal: The grown-up Alexander bumbling his way aboard Martok's Rotarran is a Mickey Mouse Club kid who went on to voice Iron Man himself.

knownFor: Beloved sitcom regular — Detective Chano Amengual on Barney Miller and neighbor Julio on Sanford and Son.
reveal: The coldly manipulative Obsidian Order operative Entek, who surgically remade Kira into a Cardassian in 'Second Skin,' is sitcom favorite Gregory Sierra buried under Michael Westmore's gray scales.

knownFor: The woman who shot J.R. Ewing on Dallas; daughter of Bing Crosby.
reveal: Quark's old flame, the Cardassian dissident professor Natima Lang, is Mary Crosby — Dallas's scheming Kristin Shepard, TV's most famous trigger-puller.

knownFor: Tony-winning stage actor and Twilight Zone leading man.
reveal: The oily Cardassian public conservator Kovat, gleefully defending O'Brien in a rigged show-trial in 'Tribunal,' is distinguished stage star Fritz Weaver.

knownFor: Tara King, the final agent alongside Steed on The Avengers.
reveal: Star Trek's first female gul — the imperious Cardassian commander Ocett of 'The Chase' — is Linda Thorson, the 1960s spy siren Tara King.

knownFor: Ubiquitous Western and genre character actor of six decades, from The Miracle Worker to V.
reveal: The Cardassian legate Turrel who brokers the Bajoran peace in 'Life Support' is veteran Andrew Prine — who had already hidden behind alien makeup as Suna in TNG's 'Frame of Mind.'

knownFor: Genre-film heavy — Neil Gallagher in Puppet Master, plus Cutthroat Island and Oblivion.
reveal: The nervous Cardassian officer Boheeka, sweating over Quark's shady deal in 'The Wire,' is B-movie fixture Jimmie F. Skaggs.

knownFor: Hapless telegrapher Horace Bing on Dr. Quinn, Medicine Woman.
reveal: The blustering Gul Dolak who menaces the Enterprise in 'Ensign Ro' is Frank Collison — TV's gentle, bumbling Horace Bing under a Cardassian scowl.

knownFor: Character actor with credits ranging from The Shining to Angel.
reveal: The smug Cardassian negotiator Gul Lemec sparring with Picard in 'Chain of Command' is John Durbin — who had earlier played the reptilian Selay delegate Ssestar in 'Lonely Among Us.'

knownFor: The actor who twice inherited Tom Hanks's roles — in Splash, Too and the Nothing in Common series.
reveal: The Cardassian defector Lasaran, whose rescue Worf sacrifices in 'Change of Heart,' is Todd Waring, Hollywood's onetime go-to Tom Hanks replacement.

knownFor: Veteran Latino character actor with more than 130 screen credits.
reveal: As Gul Macet's aide Glinn Telle in 'The Wounded,' Marco Rodríguez helped debut the very first Cardassian makeup ever seen on screen — after earlier appearing as the holographic Captain Rice.

knownFor: Bella's dad Charlie Swan in the Twilight films and the lead of NBC's Revolution.
reveal: In his very first television role, the fresh-faced young Cardassian soldier Ari in 'Second Skin' is a 27-year-old Billy Burke — nearly two decades before Twilight.

knownFor: Julie in Wes Craven's New Nightmare, later Boardwalk Empire and MTV's Scream.
reveal: The second of three actresses to play Dukat's half-Bajoran daughter Ziyal, Tracy Middendorf lasted a single episode — reportedly because she couldn't tolerate the Cardassian makeup.

knownFor: Soap-opera lead Emily Stewart on As the World Turns.
reveal: The third and definitive Tora Ziyal — Dukat's doomed hybrid daughter across six DS9 episodes — is former soap star Melanie Smith beneath the blended Cardassian-Bajoran features.

knownFor: The 'hey, it's that guy' character actor — the snooty hotel manager in Ghostbusters, plus Titanic and Buffy.
reveal: The paranoid Malcorian minister Krola, who would rather die than admit aliens are real in 'First Contact,' is Michael Ensign — who turned up as a different species in nearly every Star Trek series.

knownFor: Prolific video-game voice actor, best known as Mafia II's mob soldier Vito Scaletta.
reveal: The vengeful young Klingon Toral, scheming for the Sword of Kahless, is Rick Pasqualone — later the growling voice behind countless gangsters and soldiers in games from Mafia to Call of Duty.

knownFor: British stand-up comedian and co-creator/star of the CBS sitcom Bob Hearts Abishola.
reveal: Starfleet Academy's half-Klingon, half-Jem'Hadar War College master Lura Thok is comedian Gina Yashere — who says she didn't even realize she was auditioning for Star Trek.

knownFor: English actor and voice artist, Theodore Groves in the Pirates of the Caribbean films.
reveal: The Cardassian resistance officer Ekoor at Damar's side in the series finale is Greg Ellis, otherwise a prolific screen and video-game voice actor.

knownFor: Cuban-American actress from Nurses, The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air and Grimm.
reveal: The unflappable Vulcan Maquis gunrunner Sakonna, who mind-melds with Gul Dukat, is Bertila Damas — who returned to Trek as the liberated Borg Marika on Voyager.

knownFor: Thomas Cromwell in The Tudors and Franklin Mott in True Blood.
reveal: The third actor to play Spock's father, the pointy-eared ambassador Sarek of Discovery, is English star James Frain.

knownFor: Character actor and son of Voyager producer Jeri Taylor and sportscaster Dick Enberg.
reveal: Voyager's composed Vulcan engineer Ensign Vorik — and, as an in-joke, his 'twin' Taurik over on TNG — is Alexander Enberg, whose own mother ran the writers' room.

knownFor: Comedian, musician and writer; co-host of the Star Trek podcast The Pod Directive.
reveal: The wisecracking Ensign Mariner who anchored five seasons of Lower Decks exists only as the voice of comedian-writer Tawny Newsome — who stepped in front of the camera as Mariner exactly once.

knownFor: Voice actress and singer, the raspy voice of the Crystal Gem Amethyst on Steven Universe.
reveal: The tomboyish purple Gem Amethyst is Korean-born adoptee Michaela Dietz — who later beamed into Star Trek as the Vulcan cadet Maj'el on Prodigy.

knownFor: Veteran television leading man best known as Chase Gioberti on the primetime soap Falcon Crest.
reveal: Under the swept brows and pointed ears of Vulcan's corrupt Administrator V'Las is Robert Foxworth, the same actor who had earlier played the human Admiral Leyton plotting a Starfleet coup on Deep Space Nine.

knownFor: Character actor seen in Barbershop, Creepshow 2, and several John Sayles films.
reveal: The mustard-skinned Vulcan-Talaxian hybrid Tuvix, a man fused from two crew members by a transporter accident, is Tom Wright buried under a full-face appliance blending Tuvok and Neelix.

knownFor: Stage and screen actor who played the title role in Broadway's The Elephant Man.
reveal: The scarred, voiceless Bajoran resistance leader Orta, his face ravaged by Cardassian torture, is Jeff Hayenga under heavy disfigurement makeup.

knownFor: Actress known for Starship Troopers, Saw, and Birds of Prey.
reveal: Romulan Commander Donatra, who turns her warbirds against Shinzon to save the Enterprise, is Dina Meyer behind the ridged brow and upswept ears.

knownFor: Broadway actor and Drama Desk nominee for playing Emperor Joseph II in Amadeus.
reveal: Romulan General Movar, co-conspirator with Sela in the Klingon civil war, is stage actor Nicholas Kepros under Romulan prosthetics.

knownFor: Actress who originated Lt. Tasha Yar on The Next Generation, and a granddaughter of Bing Crosby.
reveal: The icy half-Romulan Commander Sela is Denise Crosby returning in Romulan ears and cold blonde severity, cast as Tasha Yar's own daughter from a captured timeline.

knownFor: Soap-opera actress known as Fay Walker on Days of Our Lives.
reveal: The Romulan officer Nevala, second in command to Rekar aboard a hijacked prototype ship, is Valerie Wildman under the Star Empire's brow ridge.

knownFor: Character actor best known as Mike Novick on 24.
reveal: Romulan Commander Suran, the general who backs Shinzon's coup before being forced to switch sides, is Jude Ciccolella beneath the ridged forehead.

knownFor: Six-decade character actor and one of only three performers to appear in both the original series and Enterprise.
reveal: Romulan Sub-Commander Tal, who nearly captures Kirk in 'The Enterprise Incident,' is Jack Donner under Fred Phillips' original Romulan makeup.

knownFor: Gaunt-faced screen villain whose career was bookended by Best Picture winners In the Heat of the Night and Unforgiven.
reveal: The Romulan Commander Thei, who confronts the Enterprise-D in 'The Neutral Zone,' is hatchet-faced heavy Anthony James hidden under Romulan brow and ears.

knownFor: Prolific character actor from Watchmen, Pontypool, and A History of Violence.
reveal: Senator Vreenak, who sneers the immortal 'It's a faaaake!' before Sisko's scheme dooms him, is Stephen McHattie under the Romulan ridge.

knownFor: Longtime company actor at Minneapolis's Guthrie Theatre.
reveal: Romulan General Velal, who commands the Star Empire's fleet in the final push against the Dominion, is Guthrie stage veteran Stephen Yoakam under Romulan makeup.

knownFor: Tony-nominated stage actor who played John Merrick in The Elephant Man on Broadway.
reveal: The gentle Vedek Bareil, Kira's lover and a candidate to become Bajor's Kai, is Philip Anglim under the Bajoran nose ridges.

knownFor: Character actor seen in Terminator 2 and dozens of westerns and TV dramas.
reveal: The mute Bajoran farmer Baltrim who resists evacuation of his moon, and later the foster father Proka Migdal, are both Terrence Evans behind the ridged nose.

knownFor: Star of the sitcom Wings and Jack Torrance in the TV miniseries of The Shining.
reveal: The Bajoran coup officer Colonel Day, who nearly executes Kira during the Circle's takeover, is Steven Weber taking a detour from Wings under a Bajoran nose.

knownFor: Gruff character actor best known as Piney Winston on Sons of Anarchy.
reveal: The one-armed Bajoran resistance fighter Furel, Kira's old comrade from the occupation, is William Lucking under Bajoran makeup.

knownFor: Television actor known as Robert Maxwell in the sci-fi miniseries V.
reveal: General Hazar, leader of the displaced Skrreean refugees seeking a homeworld, is Michael Durrell under a bald, ridged alien appliance.

knownFor: Actress known as Jackie Taylor on Beverly Hills, 90210, later ordained an Episcopal priest.
reveal: Nurse Jabara, Dr. Bashir's recurring Bajoran assistant in the infirmary, is Ann Gillespie behind the nose ridges.

knownFor: Character actor once shortlisted to play Captain Picard, and father of Suits star Gabriel Macht.
reveal: General Krim, the Bajoran militia commander who seizes the station during the Circle's coup, is Stephen Macht under Bajoran makeup.

knownFor: Played Tony in the 1961 film West Side Story and Ben Horne on Twin Peaks.
reveal: The reluctant Bajoran war hero Li Nalas, a legend who never felt he earned it, is Richard Beymer, West Side Story's Tony, behind the ridged nose.

knownFor: Character actor best known as Deputy Commissioner William Rawls on The Wire.
reveal: The Bajoran general Lenaris Holem, who sides with resistance hero Shakaar against the provisional government, is John Doman, The Wire's Rawls, under a Bajoran nose.

knownFor: Daytime-soap actress known as Skye Chandler across three ABC dramas.
reveal: Neela, the Bajoran engineer unmasked as an assassin in 'In the Hands of the Prophets,' is soap star Robin Christopher behind the nose ridges.

knownFor: Prolific video-game and animation voice actress.
reveal: The voice of Mass Effect's Gunnery Chief Ashley Williams, and of Batman's Barbara Gordon, is Kimberly Brooks, who also voiced Romulan Commander Kaseth in Star Trek: Prodigy.

knownFor: Prolific, rubber-faced American character actor of oddball supporting roles in Repo Man, Batman, and Something Wild.
reveal: Under the orange skull, snaggle teeth and giant lobes of the Ferengi Berik is Tracey Walter, the Saturn-winning Miller of Repo Man and Bob the Goon at the Joker's side. He liked the ears enough to return as a second Ferengi, Kayron.

performances.production: Star Trek: The Next Generation — Rascals

knownFor: Actor-turned-director, son of All in the Family's Jean Stapleton, later a busy television director.
reveal: Behind the blue skin, bulbous cranium and chin-mounted breathing apparatus of the Benzite Mordock is John Putch, Jean Stapleton's son and the Sean Brody of Jaws 3-D, who became the first TNG actor to play two Benzites when he came back as Mendon.

knownFor: Tony-winning theatrical clown, MacArthur Fellow, and Mr. Noodle on Sesame Street's Elmo's World.
reveal: The fragile, childlike Kelpien Su'Kal, cause of the Burn, is Bill Irwin under full Kelpien prosthetics, the MacArthur 'genius' clown that a generation of toddlers knows only as the silent Mr. Noodle.

knownFor: Saturday Night Live cast member (1999-2006) and creator of the deadpan 'Debbie Downer'.
reveal: The prim telepathic alien agent Dolorex is voiced, entirely unseen, by SNL's Rachel Dratch, lending the woman behind 'Debbie Downer' to an animated Star Trek spy who is herself the buzzkill of her team.

knownFor: A prolific, award-winning Japanese voice actress and singer who has headlined a decade of hit anime.
reveal: Spy x Family's lethal, breathy assassin-mother Yor Forger is Saori Hayami — the same voice behind Demon Slayer's Shinobu Kocho and A Silent Voice's Shoko Nishimiya. The kaiju census caught her voicing the hero's mother in the Godzilla anime trilogy.

portrait.author: Dick Thomas Johnson from Tokyo, Japan

knownFor: A veteran Japanese voice actor and the regular Japanese dub voice of Brad Pitt, with a long roster of anime and game roles.
reveal: The god-complex calm of Naruto's Pain belongs to Kenyu Horiuchi — also Metal Gear Solid's Raiden and One Piece's Kinemon.

knownFor: A prolific American voice actor across anime, video games, and animation.
reveal: Behind the purring, card-flicking menace of Hunter × Hunter's painted-faced Hisoka is Keith Silverstein — also Genshin Impact's Zhongli, Overwatch's Torbjörn, and Miraculous's Hawk Moth.

knownFor: A British-born Canadian voice actor, one of three voice-acting Dobson brothers based in Vancouver.
reveal: The shape-shifting spider-demon Naraku of Inuyasha is Paul Dobson — also Ranma ½'s lecherous Happosai and Lego Ninjago's Sensei Wu.

knownFor: An American stage director and prolific voice actor, Disney's official voice of Jiminy Cricket since 2014.
reveal: The tiny top-hatted conscience of Pinocchio — Disney's Jiminy Cricket since 2014 — is Joe Ochman, who also dubbed his way through the entire Godzilla anime trilogy.

knownFor: Prolific Australian-Canadian voice actor credited with thousands of characters across Western animation and anime dubs.
reveal: The snarling, honor-bound Predacon warrior Dinobot was Scott McNeil — who simultaneously voiced Rattrap, Waspinator and Silverbolt in the same series, the work he calls the achievement he is proudest of.

knownFor: American anime voice actor and voice director known for leading-man roles across Funimation dubs.
reveal: The fire-breathing dragon slayer Natsu of Fairy Tail is Todd Haberkorn — the same voice hiding inside Soul Eater's Death the Kid and Sgt. Frog's Keroro.

knownFor: American anime voice actress with over 150 dub credits, largely for Funimation.
reveal: The stoic, blade-flashing Mikasa Ackerman of Attack on Titan is Trina Nishimura, who also lends her voice to Steins;Gate's Kurisu Makise and My Hero Academia's Kyoka Jiro.

knownFor: Veteran British puppeteer of Spitting Image, the Muppet films and decades of UK children's television.
reveal: The mangy, cantankerous Hartley Hare of Pipkins was operated and voiced entirely by Nigel Plaskitt — the same hands behind the PG Tips / ITV Digital monkey.

designer: Brian Froud / Jim Henson's Creature Shop
knownFor: Percy Edwards was a celebrated English animal impersonator who could mimic over 600 bird species and built a decades-long BBC career being creatures the audience never saw.
reveal: The yipping, snarling little furball Fizzgig, physically puppeteered on set by Dave Goelz, gets its entire vocal life from Percy Edwards — the veteran animal-sound man who was every unseen bird and beast on British radio and film.

designer: Brian Froud / Jim Henson's Creature Shop
knownFor: David Shaughnessy is an English actor turned American daytime-television producer (The Young and the Restless) and the younger brother of The Nanny's Charles Shaughnessy.
reveal: The gallant, lisping fox-terrier knight Sir Didymus — a puppet operated by Dave Goelz — speaks entirely in the voice of David Shaughnessy, who also voiced the talking Hat and sundry goblins in his screen debut before running soap operas.

knownFor: Mitsuo Iwata is a veteran Japanese voice actor best known as Shotaro Kaneda in the anime landmark Akira and Orbot in the Sonic the Hedgehog games.
reveal: The cackling Deathgalien general Quval, scheming across a full year of Doubutsu Sentai Zyuohger, is voiced by Mitsuo Iwata — the same throat that screamed 'Tetsuo!' as Kaneda, hidden inside the alien villain while a separate suit actor moves the costume.

knownFor: One of Aoni Production's most prolific leading men, voicing heroes across four decades of anime and games.
reveal: The teenage Saiyan Trunks who slices Frieza in half is Takeshi Kusao — the same voice as Slam Dunk's Sakuragi and Record of Lodoss War's Parn, who also hides behind a bench of Super Sentai monsters.

knownFor: A deep, gravel-voiced Aksent seiyuu who specializes in hulking villains and strongmen.
reveal: The 'strongest creature on earth' Yujiro Hanma growls in the voice of Kenji Nomura, who was also credited behind Gaoranger's Hell's Gate Prisoner Harbal.

knownFor: The Japanese voice of teen detective Hajime Kindaichi and of SpongeBob SquarePants.
reveal: The boyish sleuth Kindaichi — and Japan's own SpongeBob — was Taiki Matsuno (1967–2024), who quietly voiced Gaoranger's Clock Org between marquee roles.

knownFor: An Osaka-born Aoni seiyuu of earnest leads and gruff supporting men.
reveal: Planetes' space-junk dreamer Hachimaki was Kazunari Tanaka (1967–2016); his census credit here is a throwaway Gaoranger Freezer Org.

knownFor: The long-running voice of One Piece's archaeologist Nico Robin.
reveal: Behind the Straw Hats' cool, deadly Nico Robin is Yuriko Yamaguchi — who also slipped, unseen, into an Abaranger Trinoid.

knownFor: A seiyuu who gave voice to a menagerie of Ash's early Pokémon.
reveal: Ash's beloved Butterfree — and his Caterpie and Krabby — chirped in Rikako Aikawa's voice, the same performer hidden inside an Abaranger Trinoid.

knownFor: The Japanese voice of Sonic the Hedgehog since 1998.
reveal: Sega's blue blur has spoken in Jun'ichi Kanemaru's voice for over 25 years — the same actor credited behind an Abaranger Trinoid monster.

knownFor: A veteran character seiyuu of young boys and old women across Chibi Maruko-chan and Sazae-san.
reveal: The mischievous little boy Yamada in Chibi Maruko-chan was Keiko Yamamoto (1940–2024), a five-decade voice actress who slipped into a Sentai monster along the way.

knownFor: A basso-profundo seiyuu famous for booming strongmen, from Robin Mask to Tekken's Heihachi.
reveal: The blustering World Champion Mr. Satan thundered in Daisuke Gōri's unmistakable voice (1952–2010), which also boomed behind assorted Super Sentai monsters.

knownFor: The voice of Yu-Gi-Oh!'s Jonouchi and Prince of Tennis' Eiji Kikumaru.
reveal: Yugi's hot-headed pal Jonouchi was Hiroki Takahashi, who was also credited behind a Power Rangers-era monster suit.

knownFor: An Aoni Production seiyuu of suave villains and warriors.
reveal: Frieza's elegant henchman Zarbon and One Piece's Absalom are Hiroaki Miura, who also lent his voice to Magiranger's Hades Beastman Chimera.

knownFor: An 81 Produce seiyuu and prolific dub artist.
reveal: The Fat Controller's Japanese voice belongs to Kan Tanaka — the same actor who disappeared into an Abaranger Trinoid.

knownFor: An Aoni utility seiyuu who took over narrating TV Tackle after Daisuke Gōri's death.
reveal: Metal Gear's luckless, dysentery-prone guard Johnny Sasaki is Naoki Imamura, a steady voice behind Gaoranger and Go-Busters monsters.

knownFor: An Aoni seiyuu of loyal knights and heavies, also Dragon Ball's monster Yakon.
reveal: Code Geass' devoted knight Guilford is Yoshiyuki Kono, whose census hits — Timeranger's Hacker Yuugento and a Go-Busters Domeloid — are exactly the kind of masks he vanishes into.

knownFor: A veteran actor-narrator (real name Seiji Yamane) and the voice of Great Mazinger's pilot.
reveal: Great Mazinger's hot-blooded pilot Tetsuya Tsurugi was Keiichi Noda (b. 1943), who decades later voiced Gaoranger's Chrismisery Org.

knownFor: A jobbing seiyuu and dub artist — the Japanese voice of Mad Max: Fury Road's Organic Mechanic.
reveal: Kyoryuger's cackling Debo Monsters were voiced by Hajime Iijima, the same throat that dubs the grease-caked Organic Mechanic in Mad Max.

knownFor: The longtime Japanese dub voice of Shaggy Rogers and of Jonah Hill.
reveal: Zoinks — Japan's Shaggy is Toshiharu Sakurai (81 Produce), who also vanished into an Abaranger Trinoid.

knownFor: A stage-troupe leader, actor and seiyuu, and the voice of Dynasty Warriors' Cao Cao.
reveal: The warlord Cao Cao commands in Yukimasa Kishino's voice — a performer whose census hit is a single-episode Gaoranger Bus Org.

knownFor: A stage actor and Prince of Tennis Musical (Tenimyu) idol, the original Kaoru Kaidoh.
reveal: The giant space assassin Gigantes was voiced by Naoya Gomoto — better known, unmasked and idol-faced, as Tenimyu's first Kaidoh.

production: Ninpū Sentai Hurricaneger: Shushūto the Movie
knownFor: Acclaimed Japanese stage and screen character actor (Shin Godzilla, 13 Assassins) known for an enormous range from salarymen to samurai to women.
reveal: The fire-wielding monkey-ninja demon Ashurazaru of the Zaru Clan is heard but never seen. That growl is prestige character actor Arata Furuta, in his first and entirely voice-only Super Sentai role.

portrait.author: Dick Thomas Johnson from Tokyo, Japan

knownFor: One of Japan's most prolific voice actors and the standard Japanese dub voice of Arnold Schwarzenegger, with hundreds of roles across anime, tokusatsu and film.
reveal: The earth-shaking roar of Kaido, 'the strongest creature in the world,' comes from 81 Produce veteran Tesshō Genda. It is the same throat that voices Naruto's Nine-Tailed Fox and Japan's Optimus Prime.

knownFor: Veteran Japanese voice actor and regular dub voice of David Duchovny and Tony Leung Chiu-wai, with roles spanning Naruto, One Piece and Samurai Warriors.
reveal: The toothy sawshark fish-man pirate Arlong of the East Blue is voiced by Jūrōta Kosugi. He also snarled as the demon Big Zenki and voiced Jotaro Kujo in the early JoJo's Bizarre Adventure OVAs.

knownFor: Prolific voice actor (Giyu Tomioka in Demon Slayer, Suzaku in Code Geass) and the Japanese dub voice of Robert Pattinson.
reveal: Final Fantasy VII's spiky-haired hero Cloud Strife speaks with the voice of Takahiro Sakurai. Between leading-man roles he also disappears beneath Super Sentai monsters like GoGoFive's Psyma Beast Bahamur.

knownFor: Veteran Japanese voice actor (1936-2012), the original voice of Dragon Ball's guardian Kami and of Nurarihyon in GeGeGe no Kitarō.
reveal: The green demon overlord King Piccolo, who conquers the world and spits out an egg to spawn his own heir, was voiced by Takeshi Aono. In a neat twist, Aono also voiced Piccolo's split-off good half, the guardian deity Kami.

knownFor: One of the defining anime voice actors of the 1980s Shonen Jump boom, voicing muscle-bound heroes and hard-boiled detectives.
reveal: The strangled war cries of Fist of the North Star's Kenshiro were shaped entirely by Akira Kamiya — the same larynx behind City Hunter's Ryo Saeba and Getter Robo's Ryoma Nagare.

knownFor: A child-prodigy seiyū turned one of the most versatile voice actresses of her generation.
reveal: The gentle magical-girl heroine Madoka is Aoi Yūki, a performer who slips just as easily into the demonic Tanya Degurechaff — the innocence you hear is pure vocal engineering.

knownFor: A baritone-voiced veteran cast as emperors, dictators and cosmic narrators across four decades of anime and games.
reveal: Gundam's fascist orator Gihren Zabi booms out of Banjō Ginga (real name Takashi Tanaka), the same throat that gave Metal Gear's Liquid Snake and Tekken's Heihachi Mishima their menace.

knownFor: A bass-voiced actor and narrator specializing in cold authority figures and unstoppable brutes.
reveal: Evangelion's glacial, distant father Gendo Ikari is Fumihiko Tachiki — who then swung to the bloodthirsty grin of Bleach's Kenpachi Zaraki without much changing his register.

knownFor: A suave leading-man seiyū and the regular Japanese dub voice of Johnny Depp.
reveal: One Piece's chain-smoking cook Sanji has been Hiroaki Hirata for over two decades — the same voice coming out of Jack Sparrow in every Japanese-dubbed Pirates film.

knownFor: A deep-voiced actor and the standing Japanese dub voice of Vin Diesel.
reveal: Trinity Blood's clownish-then-terrifying priest Abel Nightroad is Hiroki Tōchi — the throat Japan hears whenever Vin Diesel's Dominic Toretto or Uncharted's Nathan Drake opens his mouth.

portrait.author: Dick Thomas Johnson from Tokyo, Japan

knownFor: A towering-voiced seiyū cast as silent strongmen and stern soldiers.
reveal: Bleach's monosyllabic giant Chad rumbles out of Hiroki Yasumoto — the same voice behind Hetalia's Germany and Street Fighter's Guile.

knownFor: One of Japan's most beloved voice actresses, defining a generation of anime heroines.
reveal: For 31 years Dragon Ball's Bulma WAS Hiromi Tsuru; she voiced the character until her sudden death in 2017, after which the role finally had to be recast.

knownFor: The 'King of Anime Songs,' a rockabilly-turned-anison singer who also acted and voiced characters across anime and tokusatsu.
reveal: Behind Gatchaman's brooding lone wolf Condor Joe is Isao Sasaki — better known as the powerhouse belting the theme songs to Space Battleship Yamato, Getter Robo and dozens more.

knownFor: A voice and stage actor who has been the long-running Japanese voice of Tekken's protagonist.
reveal: Tekken's tormented heir Jin Kazama has spoken with Isshin Chiba's voice since the 1990s — a performer most players never picture behind the Mishima bloodline.

knownFor: A Rumiko Takahashi regular and prolific character-voice actor.
reveal: The hapless ronin student Yūsaku Godai of Maison Ikkoku is Issei Futamata — the same voice hiding inside Urusei Yatsura's Chibi and Kinnikuman's Kinkotsuman.

knownFor: A smoky-voiced seiyū cast as cool antiheroes and villains across a huge swath of anime and games.
reveal: Fate's enigmatic Archer is Junichi Suwabe — the identical velvet growl you hear from Bleach's Grimmjow, Jujutsu Kaisen's Sukuna and Yuri on Ice's Victor.
