knownFor: A loud, charismatic seiyū specializing in hot-blooded heroes and boisterous brutes.
reveal: Gurren Lagann's swaggering, sunglasses-wearing Kamina is Katsuyuki Konishi — later the same booming voice as Demon Slayer's Tengen Uzui and One-Punch Man's Tanktop Master.

knownFor: A warm, versatile seiyū beloved for fathers, rogues and theatrical villains until his death in 2020.
reveal: Final Fantasy XV's oily, tragic antagonist Ardyn is Keiji Fujiwara — the same voice that played Crayon Shin-chan's dad, Fullmetal Alchemist's Maes Hughes and Kingdom Hearts' Axel.

knownFor: A prolific seiyū and singer, and founder of the voice agency INTENTION.
reveal: Gundam SEED Destiny's angry young pilot Shinn Asuka is Kenichi Suzumura — also Final Fantasy VII's Zack Fair, and married to fellow voice star Maaya Sakamoto.

knownFor: A deep-voiced actor and director whose late-career fame made him one of the most in-demand seiyū of the 2020s.
reveal: Jujutsu Kaisen's unflappable salaryman-sorcerer Nanami is Kenjiro Tsuda — the same low, measured voice behind My Hero Academia's Overhaul and Yu-Gi-Oh!'s Seto Kaiba.

knownFor: A stage-trained seiyū cast as brash, energetic young men.
reveal: Bleach's tattooed, hot-tempered Renji Abarai is Kentarō Itō — the same voice as Naruto's Chōji Akimichi, one actor hiding behind two very different faces.

knownFor: An American-born Japanese voice actor cast as noble, full-throated leaders.
reveal: The blazing Flame Hashira Kyōjurō Rengoku, whose Mugen Train stand made a nation weep, is voiced by Satoshi Hino — the same throat behind the undead overlord Ainz Ooal Gown.

knownFor: A prolific seiyuu whose cool, cracked-glass delivery fits brooding heroes and unraveling villains alike.
reveal: The decaying, hand-shrouded leader of the League of Villains, Tomura Shigaraki, is voiced by Koki Uchiyama — the very same voice as Kingdom Hearts' gentle Roxas.

knownFor: A veteran seiyuu and singer with a laid-back, tactical drawl.
reveal: Naruto's cloud-watching strategist Shikamaru Nara — every sighed 'what a drag' — is Showtaro Morikubo, who also plays Persona 4's Yosuke Hanamura.

knownFor: A voice actor and rock vocalist known for gentle giants and moody princes.
reveal: The brooding crown prince Noctis Lucis Caelum is Tatsuhisa Suzuki, who also warms the screen as the gentle-giant Makoto Tachibana in Free!.

knownFor: An Aoni Production mainstay voicing hot-blooded fighters and rivals.
reveal: The gruff, treasure-guarding echidna Knuckles has been voiced in Japanese since 1998 by Nobutoshi Canna — who also swung the Dragonslayer as Berserk's Guts.

knownFor: A bass-voiced actor typecast for gravel-throated tough guys.
reveal: The Dragon of Dojima, Kazuma Kiryu of the Yakuza saga, growls with the voice of Takaya Kuroda — who also barked as Transformers' Starscream and a Boukenger villain.

knownFor: A commanding postwar voice actor and narrator with an imperious tone.
reveal: The original Japanese voice of the Decepticon leader Megatron across the classic Transformers anime was Seizō Katō, a Haikyo stalwart who kept working until his death in 2014.

production: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
knownFor: American actress who won an Independent Spirit Award for Stand and Deliver (1988).
reveal: The composed young Klingon chancellor Azetbur, Gorkon's daughter who carries her murdered father's peace forward, is Rosanna DeSoto of Stand and Deliver, sealed inside Richard Snell's ridged brow and fangs.

production: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine ("Rules of Engagement")
knownFor: A ubiquitous American character actor with credits across The West Wing, NYPD Blue, and dozens of series.
reveal: The smooth-tongued Klingon advocate Ch'Pok, who nearly convicts Worf of mass murder, is Ron Canada, unrecognizable under Michael Westmore's Klingon appliances.

production: Star Trek: The Next Generation ("Rightful Heir")
knownFor: Best known as Deacon Claybourne on Nashville and a longtime performer on Whose Line Is It Anyway?
reveal: The fervent young Klingon Divok, who kneels before the cloned Kahless, is Charles Esten, nearly two decades before Nashville made him a country star.

knownFor: Best known as icy matriarch Angela Petrelli on NBC's Heroes.
reveal: The Klingon woman Gi'ral, living in a hidden Romulan internment camp, is Cristine Rose, Heroes' steel-spined Angela Petrelli, buried in ridges and furs.

portrait.author:
Cristine_Rose_2009.jpg: Kristin Dos Santos
derivative work: RanZag (talk)

knownFor: A veteran voice actor best known as the animated voice double for Jackie Chan.
reveal: He surfaced in the census as the Klingon G'reck on the animated Lower Decks, but his real vanishing act is the cartoon Jackie Chan himself, whose voice fans assumed was Chan but was always James Sie.

knownFor: A leading Japanese voice actor and the regular Japanese dub voice of Tom Cruise and Ewan McGregor.
reveal: The serene, world-ending menace of Sephiroth's Japanese voice, the One-Winged Angel of Final Fantasy VII, is Toshiyuki Morikawa, who has spoken for him since 2002.

knownFor: Japanese voice actor known for Taiga Kagami in Kuroko's Basketball and Josuke Higashikata in JoJo's Bizarre Adventure.
reveal: The sneering dark Ultra, Ultraman Belial, snarling through a decade of Ultraman films, is voiced by Yūki Ono, completely hidden behind the suit and the villain.

knownFor: Veteran stage and screen character actor (Gettysburg, Thirteen Days) with a booming theatrical presence.
reveal: Under the flowing wig, bronze armor and heavy brow ridges of the resurrected Klingon messiah Kahless in 'Rightful Heir' is Kevin Conway, better known as the gravelly Control Voice of the 1990s Outer Limits revival.

production: G.I. Joe: A Real American Hero
knownFor: American voice actor and stand-up comedian, billed as Chris Latta, who defined a generation of Saturday-morning cartoon villains.
reveal: The hissing Cobra Commander, the treacherous Decepticon Starscream, and the original voices of Mr. Burns and Moe on The Simpsons were all Christopher Collins (Chris Latta) — who also turned up in the flesh as the Klingon captain Kargan on The Next Generation.

knownFor: Prolific character actor, director and screenwriter whose career spanned seven decades.
reveal: Buried under Klingon ridges as the treacherous Romulan-backed Ambassador Kell in 'The Mind's Eye' is Lawrence Dobkin — a veteran director who, twenty-five years earlier, had actually helmed the original Star Trek episode 'Charlie X'.

knownFor: NBA Hall of Famer, three-time champion and Finals MVP with the Showtime-era Los Angeles Lakers.
reveal: The towering Klingon smuggler Koral in 'Gambit' — at 6'9" the tallest Klingon ever filmed — is Lakers legend James Worthy, talked into the cameo by 'Gowron' actor Robert O'Reilly on a plane flight.

knownFor: Comic character actor best known as trigger-happy Sgt. Eugene Tackleberry in all seven Police Academy films.
reveal: The wry, eyepatched Klingon warrior Leskit aboard the IKS Rotarran in 'Soldiers of the Empire' is David Graf — Police Academy's gun-crazy Tackleberry — unrecognizable behind the ridges.

knownFor: Actress who was the original court clerk Lana Wagner on the first season of Night Court.
reveal: B'Elanna Torres's fearsome late mother Miral, encountered in the Klingon afterlife of 'Barge of the Dead,' is Karen Austin — Night Court's first-season clerk — under full Klingon prosthetics.

knownFor: Daytime soap star, long-running as Olivia Falconeri on General Hospital.
reveal: The grown, quarter-Klingon Ensign Miral Paris glimpsed in Voyager's finale 'Endgame' — hybrid forehead ridges and all — is soap-opera mainstay Lisa LoCicero.

knownFor: Star of Bring It On, Bad Boys II and the series Being Mary Jane.
reveal: Before she was a household name, Gabrielle Union spent an episode fully ridged-up as the sneering Klingon weapons officer N'Garen — a role she called 'akin to winning the Oscars' for her Trekkie mother.

portrait.author:
GabrielleUnionFeb09.jpg: Gina Hughes at http://www.techiediva.com/
derivative work: Jonathas Davi (talk)

production: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
knownFor: Canadian actress, also remembered as scheming rival Solange on Dynasty.
reveal: The doomed Klingon agent Valkris, who lets herself be vaporized rather than betray her honor in the opening of The Search for Spock, is Catherine 'Cathie' Shirriff.

knownFor: Ubiquitous character actor with decades of genre credits, from the original Trek to The Twilight Zone.
reveal: The dignified elder Klingon Tumek of the House of Kozak is Joseph Ruskin — one of only five actors to appear in both the original Star Trek and Enterprise — here vanished behind age lines and ridges.

knownFor: Actor and voice artist (Being Human, Smallville's Doomsday) and a fixture of Star Wars games and animation.
reveal: The seething, resurrected Darth Maul across The Clone Wars, Rebels and Solo is voiced by Sam Witwer — who separately slipped into Star Trek itself as a Xindi in Enterprise and a Klingon in Lower Decks.

portrait.author: Super Festivals from Ft. Lauderdale, USA

knownFor: Broadway and television actress seen on ER, Bones and CSI.
reveal: Commander Kolana, General Martok's Klingon weapons officer in 'Once More Unto the Breach,' is stage veteran Nancy Youngblut, hidden under ridges and battle armor.

knownFor: Juilliard-trained actor best known as the sneering villain Xur in The Last Starfighter.
reveal: The Klingon warrior Torin in 'Rightful Heir' is Norman Snow — The Last Starfighter's Xur — this time erased under Klingon makeup rather than chewing scenery bare-faced.

knownFor: Actress from Starship Troopers, Swingers and Mulholland Drive.
reveal: The Klingon helm officer Lieutenant Synon of the IKS Ch'Tang is Blake Lindsley — the bare face from Starship Troopers and Mulholland Drive — lost under forehead ridges.

knownFor: Veteran stuntman who was Cannon Films' 1980s Spider-Man and won a Technical Achievement Academy Award for a redesigned stunt airbag.
reveal: The Klingon crewman Ortikan aboard the Rotarran is stunt legend Scott Leva — Cannon's would-be Spider-Man and an Oscar-winning airbag inventor — buried under the ridges.

knownFor: Soap actress best known as Phyllis Summers on The Young and the Restless.
reveal: The Klingon engineer Tavana of the IKS Rotarran is Sandra Nelson — who would soon become daytime's Phyllis Summers — vanished behind Klingon prosthetics.

knownFor: Cameroonian-Canadian actress, writer and director.
reveal: The Klingon matriarch L'vanna of House Kraag, raising her nomad family on Krios Prime, is Dorothy A. Atabong under a full modern Klingon head.

knownFor: American actor and singer, a former X Factor USA contestant.
reveal: Jay-Den Kraag — the first full-blooded Klingon lead since Worf and the franchise's first openly gay Klingon — is Karim Diané, completely remade under prosthetics for Starfleet Academy.

knownFor: Known for playing the one-eyed Nadine Hurley on David Lynch's Twin Peaks and the mother in Wes Craven's The People Under the Stairs.
reveal: Behind the spoon-forehead and neck ridges of Cardassian subspace researcher Ulani Belor is Wendy Robie — the eyepatch-wearing Nadine Hurley of Twin Peaks — sealed under Michael Westmore's grey prosthetics.

knownFor: Emmy-winning character actor known for thirtysomething and as the paranoid Palmer in John Carpenter's The Thing.
reveal: The war-criminal Cardassian exobiologist Dr. Crell Moset is David Clennon — Palmer from The Thing — buried beneath Michael Westmore's ridged grey appliances.

knownFor: Veteran soap-opera actress long featured as Liz Foster on The Young and the Restless.
reveal: Tain's aged Cardassian housekeeper Mila — secretly Garak's mother — is Julianna McCarthy, hidden behind reptilian ridges across three Deep Space Nine episodes.

knownFor: Swedish-born character actress known as the elegant CeCe Rhodes on Gossip Girl.
reveal: The severe Cardassian Chief Archon Makbar who presides over Chief O'Brien's show-trial is Caroline Lagerfelt — Gossip Girl's CeCe Rhodes — under a full scaled Cardassian face.

knownFor: Known for Captain Elizabeth Lochley on Babylon 5 and Cat Grant on Lois & Clark.
reveal: Cardassian engineer Gilora Rejal, who mistakes O'Brien's bickering for courtship, is Tracy Scoggins — later Babylon 5's Captain Lochley — transformed under Westmore's prosthetics.

knownFor: Best known as George Costanza's doomed fiancée Susan Ross on Seinfeld.
reveal: The idealistic young Cardassian dissident Rekelen is Heidi Swedberg — Seinfeld's Susan Ross — rendered unrecognizable beneath Westmore's grey ridged makeup.

knownFor: Known as the philandering Chester Tate on the satirical sitcom Soap.
reveal: Cardassian politician Kotan Pa'Dar, fighting to reclaim his lost son, is Robert Mandan — Soap's Chester Tate — under a spoon-headed Cardassian prosthetic.

knownFor: Known as Red Forman on That '70s Show and the villain Clarence Boddicker in RoboCop.
reveal: Terok Nor's Cardassian security chief Thrax — Odo's predecessor — is Kurtwood Smith, the perpetually scowling Red Forman, remade by Westmore's reptilian appliances.

knownFor: Prolific audiobook narrator and cartoon voice actor — the Daria ensemble, Casey Jones in the 2003 TMNT, and a bench of Pokemon.
reveal: Every roaring Hutt, clipped Imperial officer, and silky Grand Admiral Thrawn in Random House's Star Wars audiobooks is one man performing the entire cast alone at a mic — Marc Thompson.

production: Lego Star Wars: The Yoda Chronicles
knownFor: Veteran Canadian voice actor and voice/casting director with four decades in animation.
reveal: The plummy menace of Count Dooku across the Lego Star Wars specials is journeyman voice actor Michael Donovan — no relation to the faces that wore the role on film.

knownFor: Canadian voice actor — Rocket Raccoon in Marvel's animated Guardians of the Galaxy and Hot Dog in Krypto the Superdog.
reveal: The Emperor's silky evil across the Lego Star Wars universe is Trevor Devall — the same throat as a wisecracking Rocket Raccoon.

knownFor: Actor and musician — Russian President Suvarov on 24, and once a touring bassist for Foghat.
reveal: The definitive video-game Palpatine cackle — and the Emperor of the 2003 Clone Wars micro-series — belongs to Nick Jameson.

production: Metal Gear Solid 2: Sons of Liberty
knownFor: The Commish's Detective Pentangeli, and a career game-and-cartoon voice (Twitch in Toy Story 3).
reveal: Metal Gear's silver-suited villain Solidus Snake — and Star Wars' smuggler Dash Rendar — was the late John Cygan.

knownFor: Family Guy utility voice (his George W. Bush) and a fixture of Marvel and DC video games.
reveal: Hawkeye in Earth's Mightiest Heroes — and Boba Fett and Admiral Ackbar in the Rogue Squadron games — is voice actor Chris Cox.

knownFor: Voice of the animated Ace Ventura and Gintoki in Gintama; Gordon Godfrey on Smallville.
reveal: Han Solo's swagger in the Lego Star Wars cartoons is Vancouver's Michael Daingerfield.

knownFor: The Ocean-dub Vegeta and Ryuk in Death Note; a Vancouver anime-dub mainstay.
reveal: The internet's favorite power-level meltdown — Vegeta's 'It's Over 9000!' — is soft-spoken Canadian voice actor Brian Drummond.

knownFor: On-camera heartthrob — Liam on 90210 and Wyatt on Timeless.
reveal: For twelve years the definitive Anakin Skywalker wasn't Hayden Christensen but voice-only Matt Lanter, who carried The Clone Wars from padawan to the brink of Vader.

knownFor: Shaun of the Dead, Hot Fuzz, and Scotty in the rebooted Star Trek.
reveal: The bloated, sneering junk boss Unkar Plutt on Jakku is Simon Pegg, sealed inside Neal Scanlan's animatronic blubber as a favor to friend J.J. Abrams.

knownFor: Tony-nominated as Boy George in Taboo and Broadway's longest-running King George III in Hamilton.
reveal: That regal Broadway tenor performed tens of thousands of lines as the player's Sith Inquisitor in The Old Republic and narrates the Star Wars novels — Euan Morton, unseen.

knownFor: Doctor Neo Cortex across the Crash Bandicoot games; Goemon in Lupin the Third.
reveal: Crash Bandicoot's big-headed mad scientist — and Han Solo and Poe Dameron in Star Wars: Galaxy of Adventures — is Lex Lang.

knownFor: American voice actress across animation and video games, foremost inside the Star Wars galaxy itself.
reveal: The animated Padme Amidala — serene, then heartbroken — across every season of The Clone Wars is voice actress Catherine Taber.

knownFor: One of animation's busiest voices — the DeVille twins on Rugrats, Lola Bunny, and Dexter's Mom.
reveal: Both DeVille twins on Rugrats are one actress, Kath Soucie — who also gave Mon Mothma her steel in Star Wars Rebels.

knownFor: An official Disney voice of Goofy in the 1980s and Colonel Shikishima in the classic Akira dub.
reveal: That gawky 'gawrsh' of the era — and the chirp of the original Furby — were both the late deep-bench voice man Tony Pope.

knownFor: Sin City, Rent, and Claire Temple across Marvel's Netflix shows.
reveal: Under orange Togruta skin, white facial markings, and a crown of montrals and lekku, live-action Ahsoka Tano is Rosario Dawson.

knownFor: Distinguished American actor (Tom Robinson in To Kill a Mockingbird; Admiral Cartwright and Joseph Sisko in Star Trek).
reveal: When NPR turned Star Wars into radio drama, the Dark Lord's voice wasn't James Earl Jones. It was Brock Peters, who breathed menace into Darth Vader on the airwaves across three adaptations over fifteen years.

production: Star Wars video games / Vader Immortal
knownFor: TV actor best known as Cmdr. Sturgis Turner on JAG.
reveal: For a quarter-century of Star Wars games, up through Jedi: Fallen Order and the Vader Immortal VR series, the growl of Darth Vader was Scott Lawrence, LucasArts' longtime sound-alike double for James Earl Jones.

knownFor: Co-creator of the web series Chad Vader: Day Shift Manager.
reveal: The Wisconsin YouTuber who parodied Vader as a grumbling grocery-store shift manager did it so convincingly that Lucasfilm hired him for the real thing: Matt Sloan has voiced Darth Vader in Star Wars games since Soulcalibur IV and The Force Unleashed.
